evals.name: debug-502-bad-gateway
evals.prompt: my uncloud service 'webapp' at https://app.example.com is returning 502 bad gateway but it was working yesterday. how do i debug this step by step? give me the commands to run in order, oldest-first.
evals.expected_output: Outside-in debug sequence: uc ls / uc ps webapp to check replica status and health, uc logs -n 200 webapp to see recent logs, uc caddy config to see what Caddy thinks the upstreams are (healthy containers should show up in the reverse_proxy block), uc inspect webapp for detail, uc wg show if cross-machine, uc machine ls to confirm machines are up. Explains that unhealthy containers are automatically removed from Caddy upstreams, so 502 often means all replicas went unhealthy.

assertions.text: Recommends uc ps (or uc ps webapp) to check replica/health state
Recommends uc logs to inspect recent logs
Recommends uc caddy config to see Caddy's view of upstreams
Explains that unhealthy replicas are removed from Caddy automatically
Provides commands in a sequential order

evals.name: tail-logs-specific-machine
evals.prompt: We have a replicated service `api` with 6 replicas spread across 3 machines (m1 m2 m3). One user reported a weird error at around 14:32 local time today. I only care about logs from m2 for the api service between 14:30 and 14:40 — I don't want the noise from the other two machines. what's the exact uc logs command?
evals.expected_output: uc logs api -m m2 --since '14:30' --until '14:40' or similar. May need to show the RFC 3339 form (e.g. --since 2025-12-22T14:30:00 --until 2025-12-22T14:40:00) since 'today' date needs to be inferred. The -m flag filters by machine. --since and --until take RFC 3339 or relative duration.

assertions.text: Uses uc logs command with api service name
Uses -m m2 (or --machine m2) to filter by machine
Uses --since flag with a time value
Uses --until flag to bound the end of the window

evals.name: exec-into-specific-replica
evals.prompt: I have a service called `worker` with 5 replicas. `uc ps` shows one replica named worker-d4f2 on machine vps2 is stuck in a weird state. I want to open an interactive shell inside THAT SPECIFIC container (not a random replica) to look around. How?
evals.expected_output: uc exec --container d4f2 worker (uses --container flag with the container ID prefix) or uc exec --container worker-d4f2 worker. Default shell is tried (bash then sh). Explains that without --container, uc exec lands on a random replica. May optionally show /bin/zsh or similar to override shell.

assertions.text: Uses --container flag with the specific container ID or prefix
Explains that without --container, a random replica is used
